content.problemStatement:
Dustin, is the head of an Intelligence agency. He wants to send a secret message SSS to his colleague.The message is a single word consisting of only lowercase english letters but he decides to encrypt the message for security reasons. He makes a string MMM of length NNN, such that after deleting a substring of non-zero length from MMM, the remaining string is SSS.

Calculate the number of all such possible strings he can form.

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
For each testcase the there is a single line which contains an integer, NNN and then a string SSS.
###Output:
For each testcase, output the number of possible strings modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

###Constraints


1≤T≤501 \leq T \leq 501≤T≤50
1≤N≤10181 \leq N \leq 10^{18}1≤N≤1018
1≤∣S∣≤1051 \leq |S| \leq 10^51≤∣S∣≤105
SSS can contain only lowercase English letters.
###Sample Input:
2

3 a

3 ab

###Sample Output:
1326

76
